title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Browser and Node Client Systems · Mastery Check
summary: A cross-runtime release reviewer evaluating a changed provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch typed response and failure classifier. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
